title: SAC Jet Bombs Myaung Township — 4 Killed, 40+ Homes Burned
detail: On March 23, a Myanmar Air Force jet dropped four bombs on Myaung Township in Sagaing Region, killing at least 4 civilians and burning more than 40 homes. A PDF member told DVB that casualties could rise as people remained trapped in rubble. Myaung is a township in the Sagaing Region heartland that has been repeatedly targeted over four years of conflict. The attack continues a near-daily pattern of SAC aerial bombardment against populated areas.

title: Junta Deploys Suicide Drones Against Mone Township — Monastery Damaged
detail: On March 23, two junta suicide drones launched by Light Infantry Battalion 439 struck Noboekhi village in Mone Township, Bago Region (near KNU Kler Lwee Htoo District). The drones damaged a monastery. No civilian casualties were confirmed in this specific strike, but the attack represented the continuing expansion of junta drone warfare against villages in Karen/Bago border areas traditionally under KNU protection. The junta has increasingly used small suicide drones as a lower-cost supplement to jet airstrikes across multiple fronts.

title: Arakan Army Launches Major Assault on Sittwe Naval Base — 30–40 Junta Troops Killed
detail: On March 23, the Arakan Army launched a major coordinated assault on the Shwe Min Gan Naval Base near Sittwe at approximately 09:00 local time, using heavy weapons and snipers attacking from Zawmatat and Ye Yoe Pyin villages across the river. In fighting that continued into March 24, an estimated 30–40 junta soldiers were killed at the base (which held approximately 80 personnel). AA fighters advanced to within 2 km of Sittwe city center, the Rakhine State capital and one of the last significant SAC holdouts in the state. The Shwe Min Gan Naval Base assault was coordinated with simultaneous AA attacks on Ann Township and Ngape Township — demonstrating multi-axis pressure on remaining SAC positions in Rakhine. The junta's Western Regional Military Command, already relocated from Ann (captured December 2024), faces near-encirclement at Sittwe.
